story.tasks: - Task 1: Prisma schema'ya Role entity ekle (AC: 3.4.1) - Task 2: Prisma schema'ya UserRole junction entity ekle (AC: 3.4.2) - Task 3: Prisma schema'ya RolePermission junction entity ekle (AC: 3.4.3) - Task 4: Prisma schema'ya UserPermission entity ekle (AC: 3.4.4) - Task 5: User entity'yi güncelle (Story 3.3'ten devam) (AC: 3.4.1, 3.4.2, 3.4.4) - Task 6: Permission entity'yi güncelle (Story 3.3'ten devam) (AC: 3.4.3, 3.4.4) - Task 7: Migration oluştur ve uygula (AC: 3.4.5) - Task 8: Integration testleri çalıştır (AC: 3.4.1-3.4.5)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**AC-3.4.1:** Role entity Prisma schema'ya eklenmiş - Fields: id (UUID), domainID (String), name (String), createdAt (DateTime), updatedAt (DateTime) - Unique constraint on [domainID, name] - Index on [domainID] - Relations: userRoles UserRole[], rolePermissions RolePermission[]
**AC-3.4.2:** UserRole entity oluşturulmuş (junction table) - Fields: id (UUID), userID (String), roleID (String), domainID (String), createdAt (DateTime) - Unique constraint on [userID, roleID, domainID] - Indexes on [userID], [roleID], [domainID] - Relations: user User, role Role - Cascade delete on user/role deletion (onDelete: Cascade)
**AC-3.4.3:** RolePermission entity oluşturulmuş (junction table) - Fields: id (UUID), roleID (String), permissionID (String), createdAt (DateTime) - Unique constraint on [roleID, permissionID] - Indexes on [roleID], [permissionID] - Relations: role Role, permission Permission - Cascade delete on role/permission deletion (onDelete: Cascade)
**AC-3.4.4:** UserPermission entity oluşturulmuş (direct assignment) - Fields: id (UUID), userID (String), permissionID (String), domainID (String), createdAt (DateTime) - Unique constraint on [userID, permissionID, domainID] - Indexes on [userID], [permissionID], [domainID] - Relations: user User, permission Permission - Cascade delete on user/permission deletion (onDelete: Cascade)
**AC-3.4.5:** Prisma migration oluşturulmuş ve uygulanmış - Migration dosyası PostgreSQL için generate edilmiş - Migration başarıyla apply edilmiş (test database'de) - Tüm unique constraint'ler ve index'ler migration'a dahil - Foreign key cascade delete constraint'leri eklenmiş - Prisma Client regenerate edilmiş

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: User Management & RBAC Permissions
doc.snippet: Complete specifications for Role, UserRole, RolePermission, and UserPermission entities with detailed field definitions, unique constraints, indexes, cascade delete rules, and multi-tenancy support via domainID.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Prisma schema models for hybrid permission assignment: Role entity with domainID, UserRole junction (many-to-many), RolePermission junction, UserPermission for direct assignment. All entities include proper indexes and cascade delete constraints.

doc.title: Epic 3: User Management & RBAC Permissions
doc.snippet: User story: As a developer, I want role ve permission assignment entity'leri, so that user-permission mapping yapabileyim. Includes hybrid approach for role-based and direct permission assignment with multi-tenancy support.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.section: Authorization Model (RBAC) & Permission System (Hybrid Enum + DB)
doc.snippet: Architecture decision to use hybrid RBAC model: Permission, Role, UserRole, RolePermission, UserPermission entities. Supports both role-based permissions and direct permission assignments for flexibility.

doc.title: Story 3.3: Permission Entity & Constants
doc.snippet: Permission entity already exists in schema with placeholder relations for rolePermissions[] and userPermissions[]. User entity has placeholder relations userRoles[] and userPermissions[]. Story 3.4 connects these placeholders to actual entities.

artifact.reason: Permission entity already exists with relations to UserPermission and RolePermission. Story 3.4 validates these relations are properly connected.
Role entity ALREADY EXISTS in schema (added in init migration). Includes domainID, name, unique constraint [domainID, name], index on domainID, and relations to UserRole and RolePermission.
UserPermission entity ALREADY EXISTS in schema. Junction table for direct permission assignment with domainID, unique constraint [userID, permissionID, domainID], indexes, and cascade delete.
UserRole entity ALREADY EXISTS in schema. Junction table for user-role assignment with domainID, unique constraint [userID, roleID, domainID], indexes, and cascade delete.
RolePermission entity ALREADY EXISTS in schema. Junction table for role-permission assignment with unique constraint [roleID, permissionID], indexes, and cascade delete.
User entity with existing relations to userPermissions and userRoles (lines 37-38). These relations already connect to UserPermission and UserRole entities.
Permission constants created in Story 3.3. Includes USERS, PERMISSIONS, FILES modules with actions (CREATE, VIEW, UPDATE, DELETE, ASSIGN, REVOKE). Ready for use in Story 3.5+.
ActionEnum created in Story 3.3 with values: CREATE, VIEW, UPDATE, DELETE, ASSIGN, REVOKE. Used in @Permission decorator.
E2E tests for admin user management (Story 3.2). Should continue passing after Story 3.4 schema changes (regression check).
E2E tests for user profile endpoints (Story 3.1). Should continue passing after Story 3.4 schema changes (regression check).

node.package: Prisma CLI for migrations and schema management
HTTP testing library for E2E tests

story-context.constraints:
**Database Schema Constraints:** - ALL entities ALREADY EXIST in prisma/schema-postgres.prisma (added in init migration) - Story 3.4 task is to VALIDATE existing entities match AC specifications - Do NOT recreate entities - they are already in the schema - Multi-tenancy: All assignment tables include domainID for tenant isolation - Unique constraints prevent duplicate assignments (e.g., same user-role-domain combination) - Cascade delete: When parent entity deleted, dependent assignment records auto-delete - Indexes: Required on domainID, userID, roleID, permissionID for query performance
**Hybrid Permission Model Pattern:** - Role-based: User → UserRole → Role → RolePermission → Permission - Direct: User → UserPermission → Permission - Effective permissions: UNION of role-based and direct permissions - No permission caching in MVP (direct DB queries acceptable for <100 users)
**Migration Pattern (from Story 3.3):** - Command: npx prisma migrate dev --name add-role-assignment-entities - Validate migration SQL before apply - Test on development database first - Regenerate Prisma Client after migration: npx prisma generate - NOTE: Since entities already exist, migration might be no-op or minimal
**Testing Requirements:** - Unit tests: Not applicable (no business logic, pure schema) - Integration tests: Validate unique constraints, cascade delete, indexes - E2E tests: Regression check - Stories 3.1, 3.2, 3.3 tests must still pass - No new E2E tests required for Story 3.4 (schema-only story)

interface.name: Prisma Client - Role Model
interface.signature: prisma.role.create({ data: { domainID, name } }) prisma.role.findUnique({ where: { domainID_name: { domainID, name } } }) prisma.role.findMany({ where: { domainID }, include: { rolePermissions: true } })
interface.description: Auto-generated Prisma Client methods for Role entity. Available after running 'npx prisma generate'.

interface.name: Prisma Client - UserRole Model
interface.signature: prisma.userRole.create({ data: { userID, roleID, domainID } }) prisma.userRole.findMany({ where: { userID, domainID } }) prisma.userRole.delete({ where: { userID_roleID_domainID: { userID, roleID, domainID } } })
interface.description: Auto-generated Prisma Client methods for UserRole junction table. Supports user-role assignment operations.

interface.name: Prisma Client - RolePermission Model
interface.signature: prisma.rolePermission.createMany({ data: [{ roleID, permissionID }, ...] }) prisma.rolePermission.findMany({ where: { roleID } }) prisma.rolePermission.deleteMany({ where: { roleID, permissionID: { in: permissionIDs } } })
interface.description: Auto-generated Prisma Client methods for RolePermission junction table. Supports role-permission assignment operations.

interface.name: Prisma Client - UserPermission Model
interface.signature: prisma.userPermission.createMany({ data: [{ userID, permissionID, domainID }, ...] }) prisma.userPermission.findMany({ where: { userID, domainID } }) prisma.userPermission.deleteMany({ where: { userID, permissionID: { in: permissionIDs }, domainID } })
interface.description: Auto-generated Prisma Client methods for UserPermission entity. Supports direct permission assignment operations.

tests.standards:
**Testing Framework:** Jest (v30.0.0) with ts-jest for TypeScript support. E2E tests use Supertest for HTTP requests.
**Test Structure:** - Unit tests: src/**/*.spec.ts (pattern: .*\.spec\.ts$) - E2E tests: test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts - Test database: Separate PostgreSQL instance for E2E tests
**Story 3.4 Testing Strategy:** - No unit tests required (pure schema story, no business logic) - Integration tests: Validate schema constraints (unique, cascade delete, indexes) - E2E tests: Regression check - existing tests (Stories 3.1-3.3) must pass - Migration validation: Manual inspection of generated migration SQL
**Coverage Requirements:** - Unit test coverage: N/A (no application code) - Integration test coverage: 100% of schema constraints validated - E2E regression: 100% of existing tests passing
**Test Execution Commands:** - Unit tests: npm test - E2E tests: npm run test:e2e - Coverage: npm run test:cov - Build validation: npm run build
tests.locations: - test/users-admin.e2e-spec.ts (Story 3.2 regression) - test/users-profile.e2e-spec.ts (Story 3.1 regression) - test/auth-*.e2e-spec.ts (Epic 2 regression) - src/**/*.spec.ts (unit tests, if any future service logic added)
tests.ideas:
**AC-3.4.1 - Role Entity:** - Test: Create role with duplicate [domainID, name] → PrismaClientKnownRequestError (unique constraint violation) - Test: Query roles by domainID → Returns only roles for that domain - Test: Role with userRoles and rolePermissions relations loads correctly
**AC-3.4.2 - UserRole Entity:** - Test: Create UserRole with duplicate [userID, roleID, domainID] → Unique constraint error - Test: Delete User → UserRole records cascade deleted (onDelete: Cascade) - Test: Delete Role → UserRole records cascade deleted
**AC-3.4.3 - RolePermission Entity:** - Test: Create RolePermission with duplicate [roleID, permissionID] → Unique constraint error - Test: Delete Role → RolePermission records cascade deleted - Test: Delete Permission → RolePermission records cascade deleted
**AC-3.4.4 - UserPermission Entity:** - Test: Create UserPermission with duplicate [userID, permissionID, domainID] → Unique constraint error - Test: Delete User → UserPermission records cascade deleted - Test: Delete Permission → UserPermission records cascade deleted - Test: Query by domainID → Returns only permissions for that domain
**AC-3.4.5 - Migration:** - Test: Run 'npm run build' → TypeScript compilation succeeds - Test: Run 'npm test' → All unit tests pass - Test: Run 'npm run test:e2e' → All E2E tests pass (regression check) - Manual: Inspect migration SQL for correct table names, indexes, constraints - Manual: Database introspection confirms all indexes and constraints exist
**Regression Tests (Critical):** - Test: Story 3.1 E2E tests pass (user profile endpoints) - Test: Story 3.2 E2E tests pass (admin user CRUD) - Test: Story 3.3 validation - Permission entity relations work correctly - Test: Epic 2 auth tests continue working (no breaking changes)
